serpapi_response.reconstructed_markdown:
For installing a Nissan home charger in Japan, the most reliable method is through a Nissan\-affiliated dealer, who will coordinate with specialized contractors like [Japan Management \(JM\)](https://www.matabee.com/ev/?wovn=en). [2]

### Official Installation Guidance

Nissan recommends a professional assessment of your home's electrical capacity before purchase. [4] [11]

- Step 1: Consultation: Contact your local Nissan dealer to discuss the [Sakura](https://www.nissan.co.jp/EV/CHARGE/), Leaf, or Ariya requirements.
- Step 2: On\-site Survey: An electrician will check your meter cupboard and electrical fuse board to determine if upgrades \(like a dedicated 200V circuit\) are needed.
- Step 3: Quote & Booking: You will receive a breakdown of costs, which typically includes the charger unit and labor.
- Step 4: Installation: The process usually takes 2–4 hours and involves mounting the unit near your parking spot. [11] [4] [8] [1] [10] [3]

### Recommended Suppliers and Contractors

Nissan Japan partners with established infrastructure providers to ensure safety and compliance.

- [Japan Management \(JM\)](https://www.matabee.com/ev/nissan/?wovn=en): A primary recommended contractor for Nissan, offering 6kW ordinary chargers from brands like Delta.
- [Panasonic Corporation](https://evcnice.com/blog/best-ev-charger-wholesale-suppliers-in-japan/): Widely used for standard 200V outlets and wall\-mounted chargers in Japanese homes.
- Nichicon: Specialized in V2H \(Vehicle\-to\-Home\) systems that allow your Nissan EV to power your house during outages. [0] [2] [7]

### Estimated Costs & Subsidies \(2026\)

| Item | Estimated Price \(Approx.\) |
| --- | --- |
| Basic 200V Outlet | ¥50,000 – ¥100,000 |
| 6kW Wall Charger | ¥308,000 – ¥352,000 \(installed\) |
| CEV Subsidy \(Charging\) | Up to ¥50,000 for outlets |
[7] [9]

Watch this walkthrough of the Nissan home charging setup and installation essentials to prepare for your home visit:

If you are interested, I can:

- Help you find your nearest Nissan dealer in Japan for a quote.
- Explain the difference between a 6kW wall charger and a V2H system.
- Check the latest CEV subsidy deadlines for your specific region.
